title: Small Business Seminar - Why Managers Often Fail to Lead - And How Not to Be One of Them
description: Tuesday, May 5th Networking 8:15 - 8:30 AM Seminar 8:30 - 9:30 AM Most organizations create a culture where personnel and staff are effective in accomplishing the organizational mission. These same individuals, however, are often ineffective when promoted to supervisors and managers in their practice of recognized leadership skills such as communication and dealing with problems and problem employees and managers. Why do effective individuals become ineffective at practicing what we all know as leadership attitudes and behaviors? This program examines factors such as training, ineffective modeling, loss of relational connection and accountability- and how to turn these factors around to cultivate effective leaders. Jack Enter has been associated with leadership development and training for 42 years. He has lectured throughout the United States and a dozen other countries and authored two books on leadership. He has served in leadership positions in law enforcement, the military, and in academia. Much of his presentation is based on information that attendees have shared informally during the breaks in his classes. Most importantly, he has done a dozen climate surveys in which he in

title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Assistive Technology
description: The world around us is always inventing! Emily Major with LegacyLink stays up to date with the latest technologies that may be able to assist those with disabilities, limitations, or anyone who is simply curious as to what possibilities are out there. Technology does not have to be “high-tech” either, it could be as simple as a magnifying glass or an adaptive shoelace. You never know what someone will invent next! Emily Major has worked at Legacy Link as their Program Monitor and Assistive Technology Coordinator for 6 years. She is passionate about helping adults age in place, advocates for a healthy aging population, and encourages clients to voice their concerns to promote positive change. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: 118th Annual Gala
description: A Celebration of Legacy & Leadership Presented by the Board of Directors of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce 118th Annual Meeting & Gala Thursday, May 7, 2026 6:00 p.m. - 9:00 p.m. The Boathouse at Lake Lanier Olympic Park RESERVATIONS ARE STILL AVAILABLE -- Contact Ellie Cardin at ecardin@ghcc.com We invite you to a lakeside evening of celebration marking the Chamber’s 118th year and honoring the leaders and businesses that shape our community. Set against the beautiful backdrop of Lake Lanier, this signature event features the presentation of Annual Business Awards, recognizing outstanding small and family-owned businesses, along with individual honors for exceptional leadership and distinguished service. The evening will also include the Passing of the Gavel, as we welcome our incoming executive leadership and usher in a new year for the Greater Hall Chamber. Black Tie Optional Sunset Cocktail Reception 6:00 p.m. Dinner & Program 7:30 p.m. $200 per person | $1,600 Table (seats 8) Sponsorships: Platinum $3,500 | Gold $2,500 6:00 PM- Outdoor Reception | 7:30 PM- Dinner and Program 230 E.E. description: Flights of Fantasy Book Club Flights of Fantasy Book Club This book club meets on the 4th Thursday of each month at 4:00 p.m. in the library’s meeting room to discuss fantasy and science fiction books. If you love speculative fiction and want to talk about it with others, this is the book club for you! In lieu of assigned reading, this group discusses books they have recently read. title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Regenerative Medicine in the 21st Century
description: Join Dr. Harlan South for a seminar on regenerative medicine and how Anewyou Aesthetics can help you reach your health goals. Dr. South’s passion for regenerative medicines stems from his own personal health struggles. After experiencing firsthand how regenerative therapies transformed his life, he dedicated his career to helping others achieve their fullest potential. This personal connection drives his commitment to delivering innovative, personalized care that truly changes lives. Expect the discussion of the Regenerative Medicine services to include bio-identical hormones, peptides, stem cells, and exosomes as well as how they inter-relate and optimize our total health and wellness. Dr. Harlan L. South 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Culture as a Legacy: Navigating Succession Planning with an Ownership Mindset
description: Looking at culture in business is a critical component of long-term success, especially when considering transitions like succession planning. Every company has a culture, and a good one boost profitability, reduces turnover and fosters innovation. By treating culture as a strategic asset rather than an afterthought, businesses can create a "Line of Sight" where every associate understands their personal impact on the company's valuation and long-term future. Even if your business right now is only you, customers and clients will appreciate your vision for how you want your legacy to look. In her role as Executive Director for the Georgia Center for Employee Ownership (GACEO), Marie Davis is responsible for establishing the network of partners and delivery of programming necessary for the GACEO to carry out its mission of the creation of new employee-owned companies throughout all of Georgia. By providing unbiased education, outreach, and technical assistance on all forms of employee ownership, business owners can objectively research succession planning options. In addition to her passion for employee ownership, Marie is also passionate about providing opportunities for all childr

title: Small Business Seminar - Stop Being the Bottleneck: How to Build a Business That Outlasts You
description: Most business owners are building something that depends entirely on them, and they don’t realize it until it’s too late. In this talk, Tess Helmandollar breaks down the 10 Pillars of Transferable Value from her book The Legacy Link , revealing what actually makes a business scalable, sustainable, and sellable. Drawing from experience and hard-earned perspective, she’ll challenge the way you think about growth, leadership, and control, and introduce new concepts from her upcoming work, Toxic Grace , that exposes the hidden habits quietly holding businesses back. Whether you plan to sell, scale, or simply breathe again, this session will show you how to build a business that works for you, and outlasts you. Tess Helmandollar is the founder of The Cognitive Creative and Growth Pro Partners, where she helps service-based and trade businesses turn chaos into scalable, sellable companies. With a background in journalism and business operations, she’s known for cutting through noise, identifying what’s holding a business back, and building the systems that unlock real growth. Inspired by her father’s HVAC business—and what was lost when it couldn’t be transitioned, Tess authored The Lega

title: Business After Hours - Love Your Story
description: Business After Hours Love Your Story Thursday, August 13, 2026 5:00 - 7:00 PM 4436 Thompson Bridge Rd Gainesville, GA 30506 REGISTER HERE Love Your Story invites you to an evening of connection, community, and a first-hand look at the future of belonging in Hall County. As a Gainesville-based nonprofit, Love Your Story creates spaces of belonging for families impacted by disability and medical complexity through free temporary housing, caregiver support programs, and inclusive community initiatives. Since 2022, Love Your Story has served families from across the country who travel to Northeast Georgia for their child’s therapy and medical care. Join us at 4436 Thompson Bridge Road in Gainesville, the future home of the Dream With Us Campus. Guests will have the opportunity to walk the newly purchased 8-acre property, connect with local leaders and community members, and hear the vision for what is being built. Throughout the evening, attendees can expect networking, light refreshments, music, giveaways, and an inside look at the four-phase master plan that will one day include universal design homes, a host home, and a gathering space designed specifically for families navigating d
